AA Gun (6+): Rapid-fire anti-aircraft gun. May react to enemy helicopter attacks as detailed by scenario rules. Older AAG systems may hit on 7+ where noted in the ORBAT.
APC: May transport one infantry or one support unit. In force selection, take an APC only with a valid transported infantry/support unit; APCs do not generate command tokens on their own.
ATGM: Counts as infantry/support for being shot at, but fires with one die only. ATGM fire is a double action (prepare, then fire), so it cannot move and fire in the same activation. May react with +1 reaction penalty, usually cannot fire under 12", and has restricted anti-infantry use (typically against units in buildings/bunkers).
ATV: All Terrain Vehicle. Uses the ATV value when making terrain tests.
Concealed Shooter: Improved launcher positioning and trajectories reduce exposure. Units reacting to an ATGM shot from a Concealed Shooter vehicle apply -1 to their reaction roll.
Fast: Fast MBT. Exceptionally mobile and uses the fast MBT movement category.
Fixed Forward: Weapon has a fixed forward 90-degree firing arc.
Grenade Launcher: Uses weapon value for penetration as listed. Against infantry/support, treat as Gun/Artillery/ATGM effects per the core effects table.
HEAT Rounds and Special Armour: Weapon values with an 'h' suffix are HEAT rounds. Armour values with an 's' suffix indicate special armour. If HEAT hits special armour, roll 2d10 for penetration and use the lower die.
LAW: Light anti-tank weapon (e.g., LAWs/RPGs). Infantry may fire at vehicles; typical range is 12" and they are not affected by Special Armour.
MANPAD: Man-portable air-defense missile. May react to enemy helicopter attacks as detailed by scenario rules.
Mortar: Indirect/direct fire support team that may only fire when stationary. First shot usually requires a higher to-hit threshold, later shots can improve against the same target area, and mortar fire inflicts morale pressure on infantry/support.
Rapid Fire Auto Cannons: Auto-cannon weapons with an 'a' suffix roll two dice to hit and use the highest result.
Recce: Recon/scout unit. Normally reacts on 3+ (affected by morale, not command tokens), and can trigger friendly reactions when enemies activate within LOS, representing early warning and tactical spotting.
Rockets: Attack-helicopter rockets are used against enemy targets using the listed hit value. Additional effects vs infantry/support follow Gun/Artillery/ATGM effects guidance.
SAM: Surface-to-air missile system. May react to enemy helicopter attacks as detailed by scenario rules.
Special Armour: Vehicle has special armour (usually marked with an 's' suffix). Interacts with HEAT rounds via the HEAT and Special Armour rule.
Tank Missile: Vehicle can use the Tank Missile tactical advantage capability.
Thermal: Thermal imagers reduce obscuration penalties. Unit ignores the -1 firing modifier for targets in terrain or with LOS over 50% obscured by smoke/cover.
ATGMs: Alias of ATGM: one-die fire, double-action launch, limited close-range and anti-infantry usage based on scenario/orbat restrictions.
AAG (Anti-Aircraft Gun): Alias of AA Gun: rapid-fire anti-aircraft gun with helicopter reaction capability.
FAST MBT: Alias of Fast: exceptionally mobile tank using fast MBT movement.
RECCE: Alias of Recce: recon/scout reaction and spotting rule.
SAM (Surface-to-Air Missile): Alias of SAM: may react to enemy helicopter attacks.
ATV (All Terrain Vehicle): Alias of ATV: uses ATV value for terrain tests.
